title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Synchronous HTTP blocks R session waiting reply. Asynchronous mode lets fire multiple requests process results arrive — useful bots poll several endpoints place orders parallel.

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Event loop: R built-event loop like Node.js Python’s asyncio. Promises resolve event loop ticks via later::run_now(). scripts vignettes, drain loop (!loop_empty()) run_now(). Shiny apps event loop runs automatically.

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: coro package provides async() await() writing async code reads like synchronous code:
code: library(alpaca) library(coro) library(later)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  main <- async(function() {   # These run concurrently   bars_promise <- md$get_bars("AAPL", timeframe = "1Day",                               start = "2024-01-01", end = "2024-01-31")   clock_promise <- md$get_clock()    bars <- await(bars_promise)   clock <- await(clock_promise)    cat("Got", nrow(bars), "bars for AAPL\n")   cat("Market is", if (clock$is_open) "OPEN" else "CLOSED", "\n") })  main() while (!loop_empty()) run_now()

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Fire multiple requests simultaneously collect results:
code: library(alpaca) library(promises) library(later)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE) symbols <- c("AAPL", "MSFT", "GOOGL", "AMZN", "TSLA")  # Launch all requests in parallel bar_promises <- lapply(symbols, function(sym) {   md$get_latest_bar(sym) })  # Collect results as they arrive results <- list() for (i in seq_along(bar_promises)) {   bar_promises[[i]] %...>% (function(idx) {     function(data) {       results[[idx]] <<- data     }   })(i) }  while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now()  # Combine into a single data.table all_bars <- data.table::rbindlist(results, fill = TRUE) print(all_bars)

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Place orders asynchronously monitor status:
code:
library(alpaca) library(coro) library(later)  trading <- AlpacaTrading$new(async = TRUE) acct <- AlpacaAccount$new(async = TRUE)  main <- async(function() {   # Place order   order <- await(trading$add_order(     symbol = "AAPL", side = "buy", type = "limit",     time_in_force = "day", qty = 1, limit_price = 150   ))   cat("Order placed:", order$id, "
")    # Check account   info <- await(acct$get_account())   cat("Buying power:", info$buying_power, "
")    # Check positions   positions <- await(acct$get_positions())   cat("Open positions:", nrow(positions), "
") })  main() while (!loop_empty()) run_now()

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Shiny, event loop runs automatically. Just return promises reactive expressions:
code: library(shiny) library(alpaca)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  server <- function(input, output, session) {   bars <- reactive({     md$get_bars(input$symbol, timeframe = "1Day",                 start = input$start, end = input$end)   })    output$chart <- renderPlot({     bars() %...>% function(data) {       plot(data$t, data$c, type = "l",            main = paste(input$symbol, "Close Price"),            xlab = "Date", ylab = "Price")     }   }) }

title: Asynchronous Usage with alpaca
text: Use promises::catch() handle errors async chains:
code: library(alpaca) library(promises) library(later)  md <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  md$get_bars("INVALID_SYMBOL", timeframe = "1Day") %...>%   function(data) {     cat("Got", nrow(data), "bars\n")   } %...!%   function(err) {     cat("Error:", conditionMessage(err), "\n")   }  while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now()

title: Getting Started with alpaca
text: Set API credentials environment variables .Renviron: don’t key, visit Alpaca dashboard. Paper trading: Always start https://paper-api.alpaca.markets test without risking real money.
code: ALPACA_API_KEY = your-api-key ALPACA_API_SECRET = your-api-secret ALPACA_API_ENDPOINT = https://paper-api.alpaca.markets library(alpaca)  keys <- get_api_keys(   api_key = "your-api-key",   api_secret = "your-api-secret" )

title: Margin Trading and Short Selling with alpaca
text: Alpaca accounts support margin trading box: Reg-T margin: 2x buying power overnight positions Day-trading margin: 4x buying power positions opened closed -day (requires PDT flag $25k+ equity) Short selling: Sell stocks don’t ; Alpaca borrows shares automatically special endpoints classes needed — AlpacaTrading AlpacaAccount handle everything.

title: Margin Trading and Short Selling with alpaca
code: library(alpaca)  trading <- AlpacaTrading$new() acct <- AlpacaAccount$new() market <- AlpacaMarketData$new()

title: Margin Trading and Short Selling with alpaca
code: info <- acct$get_account()  # Key margin fields info[, .(   equity,   buying_power,         # Total buying power (includes margin)   regt_buying_power,    # Reg-T (2x) buying power   daytrading_buying_power, # 4x for day trades (if PDT)   initial_margin,       # Current initial margin requirement   maintenance_margin,   # Maintenance margin requirement   long_market_value,    # Total long positions   short_market_value,   # Total short positions   multiplier,           # Account leverage multiplier (2 or 4)   pattern_day_trader    # PDT flag )]

code:
# Check asset attributes asset <- market$get_asset("AAPL") cat("Shortable:", asset$shortable, "

text: options, use position_intent signal intent:
code: opts <- AlpacaOptions$new()  # Find an options contract contracts <- opts$get_contracts(   underlying_symbols = "AAPL",   type = "call",   expiration_date_gte = "2024-06-01",   limit = 5 ) print(contracts[, .(symbol, type, strike_price, expiration_date)])  # Buy to open a call option order <- trading$add_order(   symbol = contracts$symbol[1],   side = "buy",   type = "limit",   time_in_force = "day",   qty = 1,   limit_price = 5.00,   position_intent = "buy_to_open" )  # Sell to close the option trading$add_order(   symbol = contracts$symbol[1],   side = "sell",   type = "limit",   time_in_force = "day",   qty = 1,   limit_price = 7.50,   position_intent = "sell_to_close" )

title: API Wrapper to Alpaca Trading Platform
text: classes accept async = TRUE, causing methods return promises. Use coro::async() write sequential-looking async code:
code: market_async <- AlpacaMarketData$new(async = TRUE)  main <- coro::async(function() {   bars <- await(market_async$get_bars("AAPL", "1Day",                                        start = "2024-01-01", end = "2024-01-31"))   clock <- await(market_async$get_clock())    print(bars)   cat("Market open:", clock$is_open, "\n") })  main() while (!later::loop_empty()) later::run_now()

title: AlpacaAccount: Account, Positions, and Portfolio — AlpacaAccount
text: Provides methods retrieving account information, managing positions, viewing portfolio history, querying account activities Alpaca's Trading API. Inherits AlpacaBase. methods support synchronous asynchronous execution depending async parameter construction.

title: AlpacaAccount: Account, Positions, and Portfolio — AlpacaAccount
text: Account: Retrieve current account details (equity, buying power, etc.). Positions: View open positions specific position symbol. Close Positions: Close individual positions (fully partially). Options Exercise: Exercise options positions. Account Config: View update account configurations. Portfolio History: View historical portfolio value snapshots. Activities: Query account activity history (fills, dividends, etc.). Watchlists: Create, update, manage symbol watchlists.

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData
text: Provides methods retrieving market data Alpaca's REST API, including historical bars (OHLCV), latest quotes/trades, snapshots, asset info, market calendar, clock. Inherits AlpacaBase. methods support synchronous asynchronous execution depending async parameter construction.

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData
text: Bars: Retrieve historical OHLCV candlestick data stocks. Trades: Access historical latest trade data. Quotes: Access historical latest quote (NBBO) data. Snapshots: Get real-time snapshot symbol's latest state. Assets: Query available tradeable assets metadata. Calendar: Get market open/close schedule. Clock: Check current market status (open/closed). Corporate Actions: Query dividends, splits, mergers, spinoffs. News: Retrieve market news articles filtered symbol/date. Screener: active stocks, top market movers.

title: AlpacaMarketData: Market Data, Assets, Calendar, and Clock — AlpacaMarketData
text: Market data endpoints use https://data.alpaca.markets default. Trading-related endpoints (assets, calendar, clock) use trading base URL. configurable via constructor parameters.

title: Build and Execute an Alpaca API Request — alpaca_build_request
text: base_url Character; API base URL. endpoint Character; API path (e.g., "/v2/account"). method Character; HTTP method. Default "GET". query Named list; query parameters. Default list(). body Named list NULL; JSON request body (POST/PATCH). Default NULL. keys List NULL; API credentials api_key api_secret. Default NULL (auth). .perform Function; httr2 perform function. Default httr2::req_perform. .parser Function; post-processing function applied parsed response. Default identity. is_async Logical; whether .perform returns promises. Default FALSE. timeout Numeric; request timeout seconds. Default 10. simplifyVector Logical; passed httr2::resp_body_json. Default FALSE. Set TRUE endpoints returning parallel arrays JSON nulls become NA atomic vectors.
